## Granola Subscription Cancellation Policy

At Granola, we understand that flexibility is important to our subscribers. That’s why we offer a straightforward cancellation policy.

### Cancel Anytime

You can cancel your Granola subscription at any time. Once you cancel, your subscription will continue until the end of the current billing cycle, which is the end of the month from your last payment date.

### How to Cancel

To cancel your subscription:

1. Go to **Settings > Billing**
2. Click **Manage subscription** to open Stripe’s billing portal
3. Select **Cancel subscription** in the portal

Or, if you don’t have access to the Granola app, you can [log in on the web](https://notes.granola.ai/settings/team-and-billing?tab=billing) to access Billing directly.

If you can no longer access your Granola account at all — for example, because you no longer have access to the email address tied to the account — contact us at [hey@granola.so](mailto:hey@granola.so).

### What Happens After Cancellation

After your subscription ends — or when a free trial expires without subscribing — your workspace is automatically moved to the Basic (free) plan. You will not be charged. You can continue using Granola on the free plan with some feature limitations. See [Subscriptions and billing](/help-center/managing-your-account/subscriptions-and-billing) for details on what each plan includes.

## What you need to know about unsubscribing

- Most subscriptions **automatically renew** unless you cancel them.
- Removing the Notta app from your mobile phone or deleting your Notta account **will not stop or cancel your subscription**.
- If you cancel, you can **continue to use your activated subscription plan until the end of the current billing cycle.**
- **Cancel your subscription at least 24 hours before the next billing date** to avoid being charged again.
- The remaining days of your subscription are **non-refundable**.

## Where to unsubscribe?

To unsubscribe, you'll need to go through the platform where your subscription was originally created:

- If your subscription was created via the **App Store**, you can only cancel it through your associated Apple account's subscription management.
- If your subscription was created via **Google Play**, you can only cancel it through Google Play.
- If your ...[truncated 337 chars]... App Store](https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT202039)
- [Cancel subscriptions created via Google Play](https://support.google.com/googleplay/answer/7018481?co=GENIE.Platform%3DAndroid&hl=en)

## Cancel a subscription created on Notta Web

1. Log in to the [Notta Web version](https://app.notta.ai/login) on your **computer**.
2. Click your **Profile image** > **Billing** in the upper right corner of the screen.
3. You'll find the **Cancel subscription** button at the bottom of your "Billing" screen. Just click it to proceed.
4. Finally, a confirmation screen asking "Cancel your subscription?" will appear. Click **Cancel subscription**.
5. When the "Payment Method" status switches from "Auto-renewal" to "Non-renewable" this confirms that your cancellation was successfully processed and future automatic renewals are stopped.

If you have a problem with canceling your plan, please reach out to the Notta team via the [in-app feedback](/hc/en-us/articles/17480242995099)."

## Creating a subscription package

When managing subscriptions for team members of your company, follow the below steps:

1. In your MeetGeek account, go to the ‘Plans’ page.
2. Choose the necessary amount of subscriptions and click ‘Buy’ next to the plan you need.
3. You will be redirected to the billing Portal, where you can proceed with the checkout.
4. After the payment confirmation, go to your User Management page in your MeetGeek account. Invite team members using their email and assign subscriptions you previously purchased to their accounts.

**Note:** You can enable the **Pay-as-you-go** option to your MeetGeek accounts. This means if team members exceed the recording hour limit available on their plan, the recording & transcription won’t stop. You will be charged $0.5 for every additional hour and invoiced at the end of the billing cycle.

5\. After you assign licenses to the invited users, their Plan ...[truncated 2496 chars]...e, the 10th of the month) no longer applies. The new reset date follows the day they joined the paid plan.

This explains a common scenario: a team member who recently accepted a paid plan invite suddenly hits their monthly cap and the bot stops joining their meetings. Their reset cycle has shifted to a later day in the month, so the next refill is later than expected.

If a team member has hit their cap mid-cycle:

- **Enable Pay-as-you-go.** PAYG covers the gap — the bot keeps joining and you're billed $0.50 per extra hour at the end of the cycle.
- **Wait for the reset.** Hours refresh automatically on the user's reset day. The exact date is shown on the Plans page.

- **Time invites for early in the month.** Inviting someone late in the month means their next reset will also fall late in the month, leaving them with little usable time before the cycle ends.
- **Enable Pay-as-you-go upfront.** PAYG covers gaps automatically and avoids the "bot stopped joining" surprise."

## Overview

Talk, don’t type. You can use Voice Mode in three powerful ways:

- **Brain dumps to notes:** Talk out loud and Mem automatically turns your thoughts into an organized note—great for lists, plans, proposals, and free‑form brainstorming.
- **Meeting notes:** Turn on Voice Mode and Mem records and transcribes the meeting, then distills the transcript into summarized, structured meeting notes.

**For best capture of meetings with Voice Mode:**

- Use the Mac or Windows desktop app instead of the web app
- Enable system audio permissions when prompted to capture both your voice and other participants

- **Edit existing notes with voice:** In any existing note, dictate additional content or give edit instructi...[truncated 6550 chars]...con (M1, M2, M3, …). If you see “device not supported,” you’re likely using an Intel-based Mac or another unsupported architecture. To check your chip:

- macOS: [Find out if your Mac has Apple silicon](https://support.apple.com/HT211814)

### Why does Voice Mode only capture my microphone on the desktop app?

Similar to the above, if you’re on an unsupported device (for example, an Intel-based Mac) the desktop app can only record your microphone and cannot capture system audio. That means it will miss other speakers on calls. On supported Windows and Apple silicon Macs, make sure system/computer-audio permissions are granted so Voice Mode can capture both your mic and the meeting audio.

Yes! You can use the same email address to create and join multiple teams. However, each one of these workspaces is on its own plan and needs to be upgraded individually.

Can I change my plan settings on mobile?

No, you can only make modifications to your Notion plan on desktop or web.

## About Notion

## A story of tools and the future of work

Hi there! If you’re reading this, you’re probably like me—spending most of your days in your office, in front of a computer.

You probably have fifteen tabs open: one for email, one for Slack, one for Google Docs, and on, and on…But have you ever thought about where these “work tools” came from? Or why there are so many of them?To answer these questions, and to explain why we created Notion, we have to travel back in time.

As people flooded to factories during the Industrial Revolution, many tools were invented to ease the management overload.Typewriters replaced illegible handwriting. File cabinets stored more information than any clerk could remember (and also won the gold medal at the 1893 Chicago World’s Fair!)Tools like these paved the way for offices as we know them today.

Fast forward to the 1950s, when computers came into the workplace.At first,...[truncated 1143 chars]...p, we are blending much of your workflow into an **all-in-one workspace**. Want a task list? A product roadmap? A design repository? They are now all in one place. You can even customize your own workspace from dozens of building blocks.Solve your problems your way, bounded only by your imagination.

Hopefully you’ve gotten a good sense of why we started Notion. Challenging the status quo is not an easy task. Check out the product! Write in! We need early adopters like you to start a movement. See you soon!

**“The best way to predict the future is to invent it.”**  
Alan Kay, computing pioneer

"<webfetch-citation source_id="le4L3pYoqCRdT8D10pwXB" />

We’d love for you to stay, but we understand that sometimes canceling your subscription is the right choice for you. Don’t worry - the process is simple, and you’ll still have access to your subscription features until the end of your current billing period.

In this guide, you’ll learn how to cancel your Voicenotes subscription, whether you signed up on the web, App Store, or Google Play Store.

1. Log in to the **Voicenotes web app**.
2. Go to **Settings**.
3. Click on **Your plan**.
4. You’ll be redirected to the **Stripe billing page**.
5. From there, you can manage your subscription and select **Cancel subscription**.

Subscriptions made through the App Store need to be managed directly in your Apple account.

Here’s how:

1. Open the **Settings** app on your iPhone or iPad.
2. Tap your **Apple ID name** at the top.
3. Select **Subscriptions**.
4. Find **Voicenotes** in the list.
5. Tap **Cancel subscription**.

Subscriptions made through the Play Store need to be managed directly in your Google account.

Here’s how:

1. Open the **Google Play Store** app.
2. Tap your **profile icon** in the top right.
3. Go to **Payments & subscriptions > Subscriptions**.
4. Select **Voicenotes**.
5. Tap **Cancel subscription**.

- Your subscription will remain active until the end of your current billing period.
- After that, your account will switch back to the free plan.

---

*Still need help? Reach out to us at \[email protected\], and we’ll be happy to assist.*"
